author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
śuna, he rasika jana, kṛṣṇa-gaṇa agaṇana,
ananta kahite nāhi pāre
kṛṣṇa jagatera guru, kṛṣṇa vāñchā-kalpa-taru,
nāvika se bhava⋅pārābāre

hṛdaya⋅pīḍita jā’ra, kṛṣṇa⋅cikitsaka tā’ra,
bhava⋅roga nāśite catura
kṛṣṇa bahirmukha⋅jane, premāmṛta⋅vitaraṇe,
krame laya nija antaḥpura

karmabandha⋅jñānabandha⋅, āveśe mānava andha,
tā’re kṛṣṇa karuṇā⋅sāgara
pāda⋅padma⋅madhu diyā, andhabhāva ghucāiyā,
caraṇe karena anucara

vidhimārgarata⋅jane, svādhīnatā ratnadāne,
rāgamārge karāna praveśa
rāga⋅vaśavartī ha’ye, pārakīya⋅bhāvāśraye,
labhe jīva kṛṣṇa-premāveśa

premāmṛta⋅vāridhārā, sadā pānarata tā̐’rā,
kṛṣṇa tā̐hādera bandhu, pati
seisaba vraja-jana, sukalyāṇa⋅niketana,
dīna hīna vinodera gati
en_translation:
Listen, O discerning people! The qualities of Kṛṣṇa are innumerable and infinite; no one can describe them all. Kṛṣṇa is the spiritual master of the world and the wish-fulfilling tree; He is the helmsman in the ocean of material existence.

For those whose hearts are distressed, Kṛṣṇa is the physician, expert in destroying the disease of material existence. For those who are averse to Him, He distributes the nectar of love and gradually takes them into His inner chamber.

Human beings are blinded by their absorption in the bondage of fruitive action and speculative knowledge; for them, Kṛṣṇa is an ocean of mercy. By giving them the honey of His lotus feet and removing their blindness, He makes them His servants.

To those engaged in the path of rules, He bestows the jewel of independence and grants them entry into the path of spontaneous love. Becoming subservient to His spontaneous love and taking shelter of the mood of paramount love, the soul attains absorption in love for Kṛṣṇa.

They are always engaged in drinking the current of the nectar of love; Kṛṣṇa is their friend and their master. Those residents of Vraja are abodes of great auspiciousness and the only refuge for poor, lowly Bhaktivinoda.
